definitions.text: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.
examples: Christmas gifts are commonly known to be wrapped in paper.

definitions.text: To enclose or coil around an object or organism, as a form of grasping.
examples: A snake wraps itself around its prey.

definitions.text: To conceal by enveloping or enfolding; to hide.

Paper or sheeting that is wrapped around something to protect, contain, or conceal it. A large sheet of self-adhesive material applied over a vehicle's paintwork for decorative or protective purposes.
A large sheet of self-adhesive material applied over a vehicle's paintwork for decorative or protective purposes.
A loose piece of women's clothing that one wraps around the body; a shawl or scarf.
